assumptions: The declared intragroup dividend gives rise to a recognised intragroup monetary receivable.
The receivable is denominated in a currency other than the functional currency of the relevant group entity.
The analysis is limited to consolidated financial statements and assumes the related foreign exchange differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation and can affect consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised foreign-currency receivable can be considered under the IFRS 9 fair value hedge model for changes attributable to foreign exchange risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 also provides a cash flow hedge model for variability in cash flows associated with a recognised asset or liability attributable to a particular risk.
